title: smoke-hot algerian peanuts tagine
description: nutty, glassy-jelly, sick-day algerian dish featuring peanut oil and a hint of smokiness. a perfect buddhist-temple-friendly comfort food.
instructions: heat the peanut oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
add the cumin seeds and let them sizzle for 1 minute.
add the peanuts and cook, stirring frequently, until they are lightly toasted, about 8 minutes.
add the coriander powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 15 minutes, or until the peanuts are glassy and the mixture is fragrant.
stir in the smoked paprika and cook for 1 minute.
transfer the tagine to a serving dish and let it cool slightly.
serve warm, garnished with chopped cilantro or parsley, if desired.
